honest_caveats: Capacity gain, NOT speed: a logical working-set bigger than the cgroup RAM cap FINISHES because only the bounded decompressed ring + reclaimable compressed page-cache are resident.
Bounded by compressibility: the win scales with the zstd ratio (~3.05x here on realistic log data). Incompressible data => no capacity gain (we say so).
Filesystem-bound throughput on WSL /mnt drvfs (not representative of native NVMe). The OOM-vs-FINISH contrast is fs-independent.
naive arm loads ALL logical bytes into anonymous RAM (un-reclaimable, swap.max=0) => real cgroup OOM; not a strawman (it is the normal 'load the dataset' move).
